usageTrace.validationUse: NONE -- WorkoutDefinitionValidator.cs has zero checks on Components (no count/contiguity/content check), asymmetric with RunLayoutValidator's active sequenceOrder check
usageTrace.bundleUse: republished verbatim, not special-cased by CatalogBundleAssembler
usageTrace.auditOnlyUse: PilotDomainContentAudit.cs AUD-207/AUD-231/AUD-219/AUD-243, PLACEHOLDER_UNCONFIRMED for all 4 v2 workouts

usageTrace.declaredIn: WorkoutComponentDefinition.cs:12; enum WarmUp/MainSet/CoolDown/Recovery/Strides; part of stable PlanCatalog.Contracts Process A/B boundary (confirmed via PublishedBoundaryTests.cs allow-list)
usageTrace.runtimeBehaviorUse: structurally part of published boundary shape, no runtime logic reads/branches on it in this repository

usageTrace.declaredIn: WorkoutComponentDefinition.cs:13; unconstrained free-text string, no schema enum unlike ComponentType

modelShapeConclusion: Process A's components model is a flat list of {sequenceOrder, componentType, intensityDescriptor} triples with no nested repetition-count, duration, or recovery-detail structure of any kind.
outOfScopeContextNote: EASY_WITH_STRIDES and LONG_RUN_PROGRESSION show a components-bearing variant of easy/long-run training exists in the domain, but as a different, non-substitutable workout key, not an alternate shape of EASY_STANDARD/LONG_RUN_STANDARD.

EASY_STANDARD.note: no components array in generated output for this key at all

LONG_RUN_STANDARD.note: structurally identical flat pattern to EASY_STANDARD

structuralEvidenceFields: componentType=TEMPO_MAIN_SET (no repetitions field, continuous-only across both occurrences)
THRESHOLD_TEMPO.differentlyKeyedIntervalVariant: TEN_K_REPETITIONS uses componentType=INTERVAL_MAIN_SET with repetitions=4 -- structural evidence the interval format belongs to a different workout key, not THRESHOLD_TEMPO

fixtureFieldClassification.classification: structural evidence, reusable catalog knowledge, already present in WorkoutComponentType
derived fixture output, not yet promoted to catalog vocabulary (open ownership question per ten-k-pilot-vocabulary-decisions.md)
user-specific generated value -- must never be promoted into a reusable WorkoutDefinition
derived fixture output, corroborates already-CANONICAL_CONFIRMED AllowedDistanceAccountingModes vocabulary, not the components question

evidenceLevelPerDecision.evidenceLevel: structural evidence only -- points toward no components array for this key, not toward confirming any specific catalog-authored breakdown

structural evidence only for generic WARM_UP/COOL_DOWN presence; no evidence (contrary evidence) for the catalog's generic single MAIN_SET/SURGE_AND_FLOAT middle component vs fixture's more specific FARTLEK_MAIN_SET
structural evidence only, same conclusion as D6
structural evidence only for WARM_UP/COOL_DOWN; additionally structural evidence the format is continuous-only for this key

componentsOwnershipPerWorkout.currentShapeReusableOrGenerated: neither cleanly -- authored placeholder not matching generated model's actual shape (which has no components for this key)
componentsOwnershipPerWorkout.belongsInWorkoutDefinition: only if a components concept is kept for this key at all
componentsOwnershipPerWorkout.leadingCandidate: optional structural descriptor, or no components requirement at all

componentsOwnershipPerWorkout.currentShapeReusableOrGenerated: structurally reusable (matches fixture's 3-part shape); middle intensityDescriptor is a stable label, not a copied generated value -- no Category-B violation
componentsOwnershipPerWorkout.leadingCandidate: component-capability taxonomy is defensible as-is; MAIN_SET granularity vs fixture's FARTLEK_MAIN_SET is an unresolved vocabulary-granularity question, not evidence current shape is wrong

componentsOwnershipPerWorkout.currentShapeReusableOrGenerated: authored placeholder, same character as EASY_STANDARD -- fixture never structures this key with components (10/10 flat)
componentsOwnershipPerWorkout.belongsInWorkoutDefinition: only if a components concept is kept for this key at all
componentsOwnershipPerWorkout.leadingCandidate: optional structural descriptor, or no components requirement at all (same conclusion as EASY_STANDARD)

componentsOwnershipPerWorkout.currentShapeReusableOrGenerated: structurally reusable and consistent with both fixture occurrences (continuous single main-set, no repetitions) -- the closest match of the four workouts to its fixture counterpart
componentsOwnershipPerWorkout.leadingCandidate: existing generic breakdown is reasonable as-is; a future interval/cruise-repeat variant should likely be a different WorkoutDefinition key (mirroring fixture's TEN_K_REPETITIONS pattern), not a reshape of THRESHOLD_TEMPO

distanceAccountingModeCorrelationNote: AllowedDistanceAccountingModes (out of scope, CANONICAL_CONFIRMED) already splits EASY_STANDARD/LONG_RUN_STANDARD (EXACT_SESSION_TOTAL) from FARTLEK/THRESHOLD_TEMPO (ESTIMATED_SESSION_TOTAL) -- the same split the fixture shows for components-array presence. This is a structural correlation, not proof of duplication (different concerns), but suggests components necessity may be derivable from DistanceAccountingMode rather than independently authored per workout.
finalStatus: Components ownership and Golden Fixture boundary analysis complete for all 4 workouts. No schema, code, or classification was changed.
